headings.usage: Use for all page titles, section headers, and data labels.

body.usage: Use for disease descriptions, disclaimers, and general text.

mono.usage: Use for confidence scores or strict numerical data to give a precise, clinical feel.

colors.notes: Maintain strict APCA contrast standards. Do not use gradients for data-heavy sections; rely on flat, crisp colors to avoid optical refraction of medical information.

visual_enhancers.spacing: Use p-6, p-8, and p-12 generously. Medical apps require massive breathing room to reduce cognitive load.
visual_enhancers.border_radius: rounded-2xl for main cards, rounded-xl for inner elements. Avoid sharp brutalist edges.
visual_enhancers.shadows: shadow-[0_8px_30px_rgba(15,23,42,0.04)] - keep shadows extremely subtle and ambient.
visual_enhancers.custom_cursor: Implement a custom doctor symbol cursor globally via CSS: `cursor: url('data:image/svg+xml;utf8,<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="32" height="32" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="%231E3A8A"><path d="M19 10h-4V6c0-1.1-.9-2-2-2h-2c-1.1 0-2 .9-2 2v4H5c-1.1 0-2 .9-2 2v2c0 1.1.9 2 2 2h4v4c0 1.1.9 2 2 2h2c1.1 0 2-.9 2-2v-4h4c1.1 0 2-.9 2-2v-2c0-1.1-.9-2-2-2z"/></svg>') 16 16, auto;`
visual_enhancers.surfaces: Use 'Flat Solid' strategy. Avoid Glassmorphism for medical data sections to ensure 100% clarity. Use 1px subtle borders (border-slate-200) to define sections.

grids.marketing_hero: MODE A: 'Tetris' Grid for the landing section. Hero text spanning 8 columns, abstract clinical image spanning 4 columns.
grids.results_dashboard: MODE B: High Density Grid. Image thumbnail (col-span-1), Diagnosis (col-span-2), Confidence Score Widget (col-span-1). Use h-full to align bottom edges.

layouts.navigation: Clean, sticky top nav with Logo on left and 'Emergency Disclaimer' explicitly written on the right. No auth buttons.
layouts.upload_section: Massive drag-and-drop zone with dashed border-slate-300, minimal icon, and clear 'Upload Skin Image' text. Must have disclaimer checkbox below.
layouts.results_page: Bento-style layout. Left side: original image with a subtle scanning overlay. Right side: tabbed or stacked cards for Disease Name, Confidence, Description, Treatments, and PDF Download CTA.

motion.hover_states: Buttons should have slight lift (-translate-y-0.5) and deeper background color (hover:bg-blue-900).
motion.page_transitions: Fade-in (opacity from 0 to 1) and slide-up (y from 10 to 0) using framer-motion.
motion.loading_state: For the AI detection phase, implement a 'Scanning' animation over the image—a horizontal blue/green tinted line moving up and down to signify analysis.

accessibility.contrast: All text must pass 4.5:1 minimum. Use blue-900 or slate-900 on white backgrounds.
accessibility.disclaimers: Medical disclaimers must be visually distinct (e.g., wrapped in a soft yellow or red-tinted alert box) and clearly readable.
accessibility.focus: focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-600 focus:ring-offset-2 on all interactive elements.
accessibility.test_attributes: Add data-testid to all inputs, buttons, and critical result fields.

media.image_usage: Use images primarily for the marketing/landing hero. For the application itself, prioritize clean UI over decorative images.

3d_libraries.recommendation: Avoid heavy 3D for this clinical app to maintain blazing fast load times and trust. Use framer-motion for simple 'scanning' micro-interactions.
3d_libraries.data_visualization: Use simple SVG progress bars or Recharts (if needed) for confidence scores, keeping styling minimal.

hero_patient_exam.description: Dermatologist using advanced technology for a skin examination in a clinic setting. Use for landing page hero.

hero_tablet_exam.description: Healthcare professional examining patient's skin using tablet. Use as secondary feature image.

instructions_to_main_agent: Install libraries: `yarn add @phosphor-icons/react framer-motion html2pdf.js jspdf react-dropzone`
Use standard JS files (.jsx/.js) as the environment does not support TSX.
Global CSS: Apply the custom SVG cursor to `body, a, button, input` to meet the 'Doctor symbol cursor' requirement.
Disclaimer Enforcement: The user MUST check a box saying 'I understand this is not medical advice' before the Upload button becomes active.
PDF Generation: Implement a feature using html2pdf or jspdf that takes the results DOM node and exports it as a medical-style PDF report with letterhead.
Color Usage: Only use the defined slate-900, white, and specific medical blues/greens. Avoid any generic 'tech' gradients.
Implement the 'Bento Grid' strategy for the results display. Keep it rigid, precise, and highly legible.
Do NOT track history; ensure all state is cleared if the user navigates away or refreshes.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: Do not converge toward generic 'AI slop' aesthetics. Use strict, deliberate spacing and typography.
Never use dark colors as gradients. Stick to flat, clinical colors.
Avoid generic centered layouts for data; align text to the left for readability.
Create depth with subtle borders and very light shadows, not heavy drop shadows.
Do not center align the `.App` container text globally.
Never use emoji characters for icons; use @phosphor-icons/react.
Ensure every interaction has a deliberate micro-animation (e.g., hover, focus).
Use 2-3x more spacing (p-6, p-8) than feels comfortable to prevent cramped layouts.
Export components as named exports and pages as default exports.
Use `sonner` for toast notifications (e.g., error during image upload).
